source: CEFR B2 descriptors + standard reference French grammar (Grammaire progressive du français, CLE International)

cards.title: Extended narrative with evaluation
cards.goal: Can narrate a complex sequence of events, weaving in background, complication and personal evaluation of what happened.
grammar: narrative tenses fully integrated: passé composé, imparfait, plus-que-parfait
passé composé passif within narrative
discourse: evaluative asides: ce qui était intéressant, c'est que…, le plus étrange, c'est que…, avec le recul…
cards.evidence: Tell a 2–3 minute story that includes at least one plus-que-parfait clause and two evaluative comments on the events, not just a report of them.

cards.title: Describing trends & change over time
cards.goal: Can describe how something has changed over an extended period and explain the causes.
grammar: depuis que + clause
de plus en plus / de moins en moins
cause-result linking: grâce à, à cause de, ce qui fait que
lexis: trend language: augmenter, chuter, progressivement, radicalement, se stabiliser
cards.evidence: Describe a trend or change (an industry, a place, a habit) in 90 seconds, with at least two causes explicitly linked to their results.
skin_examples: a personal habit that's shifted over years

cards.title: Economical description
cards.goal: Can describe people, things and situations concisely using reduced clauses instead of full relative clauses.
grammar: participe présent as a reduced relative: la femme assise près de la fenêtre
relative pronouns with prepositions: dont, lequel/laquelle, auquel
cards.evidence: Produce five descriptive sentences, at least three using a participle or dont/lequel construction where a clumsier full relative clause would otherwise be needed.

cards.title: Opinions with nuance & hedging
cards.goal: Can give a qualified opinion, softening claims where appropriate and signalling degrees of certainty.
grammar: subjonctif after doubt/negated opinion: je ne pense pas que…, il est possible que…
hedging: dans une certaine mesure, il semblerait que, on pourrait dire que
cards.evidence: Give a two-minute opinion on a debatable topic including at least one hedge, one strong unhedged claim, and one concession to the other side, with correct subjunctive use where triggered.
skin_examples: is remote work good for teams?

cards.title: Debating & structured disagreement
cards.goal: Can take and defend a position in a discussion, concede a point without losing the argument, and return to the main line.
discourse: debate structure: je comprends votre point de vue, mais…, cela dit…, tout compte fait…
cards.evidence: Take a side in a 4-minute structured debate; concede one point convincingly, then restate and defend the original position.
skin_examples: a genuinely contested topic from the week's news

cards.title: Comparing & weighing options
cards.goal: Can weigh up two complex options against each other and reach a reasoned conclusion.
grammar: plus… plus… / moins… moins…
d'autant plus que
advanced comparative and superlative structures
cards.evidence: Weigh two complex options (e.g. two career or life choices) using at least two plus-plus/d'autant-plus structures, reaching a justified conclusion.

cards.title: Persuading & recommending with justification
cards.goal: Can persuade someone toward a course of action with clear justification.
cards.evidence: Persuade the listener toward a specific choice in 90 seconds using at least three persuasive exponents and two distinct justifications.
skin_examples: convincing someone to choose an option

cards.title: Hypothesising about present & future
cards.goal: Can reflect on how past decisions shape the present, and wish things were different now.
grammar: conditionnels mixtes: si + plus-que-parfait, conditionnel présent (present result of a past condition)
si seulement + imparfait
j'aimerais que + subjonctif
cards.evidence: Produce three mixed-conditional or si-seulement sentences reflecting on a real present situation shaped by a past decision.
skin_examples: if you'd taken the other path
if only things were different right now
a what-if about where you live

cards.title: Speculating about the past with nuance
cards.goal: Can speculate about an unclear past situation with graded certainty, including ongoing past actions.
grammar: devoir/pouvoir + infinitif passé consolidated with graded certainty
il se peut que + subjonctif passé
devoir être en train de + infinitif passé for ongoing-past speculation
cards.evidence: Speculate about an unexplained past situation (a mystery, an unexplained absence) across five sentences, with clearly graded certainty.

cards.title: Unreal past & regret
cards.goal: Can reflect on a real past regret and imagine credible alternatives.
grammar: conditionnel passé consolidated
si seulement + plus-que-parfait
regret vocabulary: j'aurais aimé, je regrette de ne pas avoir…
cards.evidence: Reflect on a real past regret for 90 seconds using at least three conditionnel-passé or si-seulement + plus-que-parfait structures.

cards.title: Interrupting, holding the floor & yielding it
cards.goal: Can manage turn-taking in a group discussion: interrupt, hold the floor under pressure, and yield it gracefully.
functions: gérer la prise de parole en groupe
discourse: turn-taking markers: je peux juste ajouter quelque chose ?, laisse-moi finir, vas-y, je t'écoute, désolé(e), tu disais ?
cards.evidence: In a 3-person discussion, interrupt politely once, hold the floor against an interruption once, and yield the floor once — all naturally.

cards.title: Negotiating & reaching compromise
cards.goal: Can negotiate between conflicting priorities toward a concrete compromise.
exponents: On coupe la poire en deux
cards.evidence: Negotiate a scenario with conflicting priorities to a concrete compromise, using at least three negotiation exponents.
skin_examples: splitting costs on a shared project

cards.title: Dealing with the unexpected
cards.goal: Can respond fluently when a conversation takes an unplanned turn.
cards.evidence: Handle a roleplay in which the interlocutor introduces an unplanned complication; adapt fluently without switching to L1 or breaking down.

cards.title: Structuring a talk or presentation
cards.goal: Can deliver a short structured talk with a clear opening, signposted sections and a closing summary.
discourse: signposting: je voudrais commencer par…, passons maintenant à…, ce qui m'amène à…, pour conclure
cards.evidence: Give a structured 3-minute talk on a familiar topic with a clear opening, three signposted sections and a closing summary.

cards.title: Linking ideas across a longer stretch
cards.goal: Can connect an extended argument using a wide, varied range of logical linkers.
grammar: wide range of linkers: alors que, bien que/même si + subjonctif, malgré/en dépit de + nom, à condition que + subjonctif, à moins que + subjonctif, au cas où + conditionnel
cards.evidence: Produce a six-sentence argument using at least five different linking devices from the list above, without repeating any, and with correct subjunctive triggering.
skin_examples: a policy the student has an opinion on

cards.title: Emphasis & cleft sentences
cards.goal: Can use emphatic structures to highlight the most important part of a message.
grammar: cleft sentences: c'est… qui/que, ce qui/ce que… c'est…
inversion after certain adverbs: à peine… que, aussi + inversion
cards.evidence: Rework four plain statements into emphatic cleft or inverted forms and deliver them naturally in context.

cards.title: Passive & impersonal structures
cards.goal: Can describe processes and report information impersonally, appropriate to a neutral or formal register.
grammar: voix passive across tenses
passive with modals: doit être fait, doit avoir été fait
impersonal reporting: on dit que / il paraît que / il semblerait que
cards.evidence: Report on a process or a piece of news in six sentences, at least four impersonal-passive, appropriate to a neutral/formal register.

cards.title: Getting things done for you
cards.goal: Can describe services arranged on his/her behalf and arrange one in real time.
grammar: causative faire faire / se faire faire: je me suis fait couper les cheveux, j'ai fait réparer ma voiture
cards.evidence: Describe three services arranged on your behalf using the causative correctly, then roleplay arranging a fourth.

cards.title: Reported speech: full range
cards.goal: Can report a complex exchange precisely, using a wide range of reporting verbs.
grammar: discours indirect across a full range of tenses and moods (including subjonctif and conditionnel)
reported questions, requests and commands consolidated
reporting verbs: suggérer, recommander, insister pour que, nier, admettre, avertir
cards.evidence: Report a complex exchange (an argument or a negotiation) using at least five different reporting verbs beyond dire/demander.

cards.title: Problem-solving & complaint escalation
cards.goal: Can escalate a complaint from a calm initial statement to a firm demand for resolution, adapting register as it escalates.
functions: faire monter puis résoudre un litige
cards.evidence: Handle an escalating complaint roleplay from initial complaint through to a firm but polite resolution demand.

cards.title: Register: formal vs informal
cards.goal: Can recognise and switch between formal and informal register appropriately for the situation, in speech and writing.
grammar: tu vs vous consolidated in mixed professional/social settings
conditionnel de politesse: je voudrais…, pourriez-vous…, auriez-vous…
formal vs informal vocabulary pairs
cards.evidence: Deliver the same message (a request, a complaint, an apology) twice — once informally to a friend (tu), once formally in an email register (vous) — correctly shifting register throughout, not just the opening line.
skin_examples: asking a favour: friend vs client
